A Discounted Cash Flow model estimates what a business could be worth by projecting its future cash flows and then discounting those back to today.

data.title: ZhongAn Online P & C Insurance (SEHK:6060) Net Income Surge Tests Bearish Margin Narratives
data.description:
ZhongAn Online P & C Insurance (SEHK:6060) has just posted its FY 2025 first half numbers, with revenue of C¥15.8 billion and basic EPS of C¥0.45, set against trailing twelve month revenue of C¥36.4 billion and EPS of C¥0.70 that reflect the 82.5% earnings growth reported over the past year. The company has seen revenue move from C¥33.7 billion on a trailing basis in mid 2024 to C¥36.4 billion now, while EPS on the same basis has gone from C¥0.41 to C¥0.70, pointing to tighter earnings power built on gradually improving margins.

With the headline figures on the table, the next step is to compare these results with the dominant narratives around ZhongAn Online P & C Insurance to see which stories the numbers support and which they call into question.

Net income climbs to C¥668 million

  • Net income for 1H FY 2025 is C¥667.6 million, compared with C¥55.5 million in 1H FY 2024 and C¥548.0 million in 2H FY 2024. Basic EPS over these three halves moved from C¥0.04 to C¥0.37 and now C¥0.45.
  • Bulls argue that tech driven efficiency and higher renewal rates can support stronger profit trends, and the step up in net income and EPS across these halves aligns with that view, although:
    • Trailing twelve month net income of about C¥1.1b and EPS of C¥0.70 sit below the C¥1.2b and C¥0.83 levels seen a half earlier, so recent strength is not a straight line.
    • Forecast earnings growth of about 11.3% per year is lower than the 23.3% five year annualized rate cited in the analysis, so bullish expectations for very rapid compounding need to be weighed against these more moderate projections.
On this profit profile, bulls who see AI and proprietary channels as long term earnings drivers may want to compare these reported margins and growth rates with the fuller bullish thesis in 🐂 ZhongAn Online P & C Insurance Bull Case.

  • Critics highlight that higher expenses and competition could keep profitability tight, and the numbers partly back that cautious stance:
    • Even with margin at 3%, the stock trades around C¥13.70 per share against a DCF fair value of C¥11.35, so the price sits above the cash flow based estimate despite only modest profitability.
    • Revenue growth is expected at roughly 6% per year, below the 8.3% figure cited for the broader Hong Kong market proxy. This means the premium P/E is not paired with faster top line growth in the analysis data.
Skeptical investors who focus on expense pressure and competition may want to see how these margin and valuation figures are treated in the fuller bear case in 🐻 ZhongAn Online P & C Insurance Bear Case.

  • Consensus narrative notes that technology export growth and new insurance products could support future margin gains, and the current mix of faster earnings growth and a 3% net margin fits that story with some tension:
    • Forecast revenue growth of roughly 6% and earnings growth of about 11.3% suggest profits are expected to grow quicker than sales, consistent with a focus on efficiency rather than just volume.
    • At the same time, the stock price of C¥13.70 and analyst price target of C¥21.62 leave a wide gap that investors need to test against these moderate growth assumptions and the current margin level.

data.title: Intuitive Machines (LUNR) Q4 Loss Of US$19.9 Million Tests Profitability Narrative
data.description:
Intuitive Machines (LUNR) closed FY 2025 with Q4 revenue of US$44.8 million and a basic EPS loss of US$0.17, alongside a net loss of US$19.9 million, while on a trailing 12 month basis revenue was US$210.1 million and EPS stood at a loss of US$0.71 with a net loss of US$83.9 million. Over recent quarters, the company has seen quarterly revenue move from US$62.5 million in Q1 2025 to US$50.3 million in Q2, US$52.4 million in Q3 and US$44.8 million in Q4, with EPS losses ranging between US$0.06 and US$0.22 across the period. TTM losses of US$83.9 million keep profitability in focus

  • On a trailing 12 month basis, Intuitive Machines booked US$210.1 million in revenue against a net loss of US$83.9 million and a basic EPS loss of US$0.71, which sits alongside five year losses that have grown at about 53.5% per year.
  • Critics highlight in the bearish narrative that persistent negative margins and heavy dependence on large government contracts could keep earnings under pressure, and the current loss profile lines up with that concern as quarterly net loss moved between US$7 million and US$25.3 million through FY 2025 while still relying heavily on mission and program awards to convert backlog into revenue.
    • The move from a Q4 2024 net loss of US$149.3 million to a Q4 2025 net loss of US$19.9 million shows that recent period results differ markedly from earlier TTM loss levels, which both bulls and bears will likely factor into their views on how quickly margins might improve.
    • Bears also point to the capital intensive model and customer concentration risk, and the trailing 12 month loss of US$83.9 million alongside ongoing investments highlighted in the narratives gives that risk a clear numerical anchor for anyone watching the path to profitability.
On this view, skeptics may feel recent earnings still leave little room for error before contract delays or cost overruns start to bite into already negative margins, and they may look to how management talks about funding and contract diversification in future updates before getting more comfortable with the long term earnings path.🐻 Intuitive Machines Bear Case

High growth forecasts vs current P/S of 10.7x

  • Forecasts in the data call for revenue growth of about 29.2% per year and earnings growth of about 55.77% per year with profitability expected within three years, yet the stock is currently trading on a P/S of 10.7x compared with peer and industry averages of 5.2x and 4.6x.
  • Supporters in the bullish narrative argue that leadership in lunar missions and deep space infrastructure plus vertical integration could justify paying this higher multiple, but the current P/S premium means those growth expectations already carry a price tag that investors need to be comfortable underwriting.
    • The same narrative talks about revenue potentially reaching around US$519.5 million and earnings of US$39.4 million by 2028, which is a large step up from the trailing 12 month revenue base of US$210.1 million and loss of US$83.9 million, so any slowdown versus the projected 30% plus growth path could leave the P/S ratio exposed.
    • Analysts in the bullish cohort also assume margins move from roughly negative triple digit levels today to positive mid single digit or better in a few years, so the current valuation effectively leans on a shift from loss making to profitable that has not yet appeared in the historical EPS trend of quarterly losses between US$0.06 and US$0.22 per share in FY 2025.
If you are weighing the bullish case, it really comes down to whether those growth and margin targets justify paying roughly double the peer and industry P/S while the company is still in loss making territory today.🐂 Intuitive Machines Bull Case

  • The stock trades at US$18.91 against a DCF fair value in the data of US$49.46, implying a large modeled gap, at the same time as the risk summary flags high share price volatility in the past three months plus dilution and insider selling over the past year.
  • Consensus narrative views this kind of set up as a trade off between a potential valuation gap and execution plus sentiment risks, and the combination of a modeled fair value well above the current price with a P/S multiple already ahead of peers shows how sharply opinions can split.
    • On one side, the roughly 61.8% discount to the DCF fair value and forecast revenue growth of about 29.2% per year appeal to investors who put more weight on long term contract potential and expected profitability within three years than on recent volatility.
    • On the other side, the same data highlights shareholder dilution and recent insider selling, which some investors treat as warning signs that can add pressure in a stock that is already described as highly volatile and still posting an annual loss of US$83.9 million on US$210.1 million of revenue.

  • This development is attracting attention from shareholders as a possible turning point for NasdaqGS:CZR.

Caesars Entertainment, trading as NasdaqGS:CZR, last closed at $27.44. The share price return is up 44.8% over the past 30 days and 16.5% year to date, while the value score is currently 5. Over longer periods, returns have been mixed, including a 2.9% decline over the past year and deeper pullbacks over 3 and 5 years.

The emergence of Tilman Fertitta and Carl Icahn as competing bidders is drawing renewed attention to how Caesars Entertainment might evolve from here. Any change of control could affect priorities for its casino portfolio, balance sheet focus, and capital allocation. For existing and potential shareholders, a key consideration is how any final deal terms might compare with the current $27.44 share price and recent share price performance.

The reported discussions between Tilman Fertitta and Caesars Entertainment put the business model under a spotlight. A potential US$34 per share takeout level, versus the recent US$27.44 close, effectively forces the market to ask what Caesars is worth as an operator of Las Vegas Strip resorts, regional casinos and a growing digital arm. Fertitta controls Golden Nugget casinos, an NBA franchise and a large hospitality portfolio, so an eventual deal could reshape how Caesars competes with peers such as MGM Resorts and Wynn Resorts, particularly on cross marketing, loyalty programs and event driven traffic.

data.description:
• If you are wondering whether NBT Bancorp at around US$40.97 is starting to look interesting, the current share price and fundamentals raise some clear valuation questions worth unpacking.
  • The stock has seen a 0.7% decline over the last week, a 10.4% decline over the past month, and a 1.5% decline year to date, while the 1 year, 3 year, and 5 year returns stand at a 1.9% decline, 34.7%, and 21.3% respectively.
  • Recent attention on regional banks, regulatory capital expectations, and investor focus on balance sheet strength has kept sentiment around the sector in the spotlight. Against that backdrop, NBT Bancorp's share price shifts are easier to understand when viewed alongside its fundamentals and perceived quality as a regional bank.
  • NBT Bancorp currently holds a valuation score of 5/6. The rest of this article will walk through the main valuation approaches behind that number, before finishing with a way to go beyond the checklist and build a fuller view of what the stock might be worth.

Find out why NBT Bancorp's -1.9% return over the last year is lagging behind its peers.

Advertisement

Approach 1: NBT Bancorp Excess Returns Analysis

The Excess Returns model looks at how much profit a bank can earn above its cost of equity, then capitalizes those extra profits into an estimated per share value. It is less about raw earnings size and more about what those earnings represent relative to the equity invested.

For NBT Bancorp, the key inputs are:

  • Book Value: $36.32 per share
  • Stable EPS: $4.54 per share (source, weighted future Return on Equity estimates from 5 analysts)
  • Cost of Equity: $2.83 per share
  • Excess Return: $1.71 per share
  • Average Return on Equity: 11.18%
  • Stable Book Value: $40.56 per share (source, weighted future Book Value estimates from 6 analysts)

The model converts these excess returns into an intrinsic value of about $88.35 per share. Against a current price around $40.97, this implies a 53.6% discount, suggesting NBT Bancorp screens as materially undervalued on this approach.

Result: UNDERVALUED

Our Excess Returns analysis suggests NBT Bancorp is undervalued by 53.6%. Track this in your watchlist or portfolio, or discover 49 more high quality undervalued stocks.

Head to the Valuation section of our Company Report for more details on how we arrive at this Fair Value for NBT Bancorp.

Approach 2: NBT Bancorp Price vs Earnings

For profitable companies like NBT Bancorp, the P/E ratio is a straightforward way to connect what you pay per share with what the bank earns per share. It gives you a quick sense of how many dollars investors are currently willing to pay for one dollar of earnings.

What counts as a reasonable P/E often reflects two things: how quickly earnings are expected to change over time and how risky those earnings are perceived to be. Higher expected growth or lower perceived risk can support a higher P/E, while more uncertainty or weaker growth expectations can justify a lower one.

NBT Bancorp currently trades on a P/E of 12.64x. That compares with an industry average P/E for Banks of 11.11x and a peer average of 14.36x, which puts NBT Bancorp between the broader sector and closer peers. Simply Wall St’s Fair Ratio for NBT Bancorp is 13.34x, a proprietary estimate of what the P/E might be given factors such as earnings characteristics, industry, profit margins, market cap and risk profile. Because it adjusts for these elements rather than relying only on simple peer or industry comparisons, it can be a more tailored reference point. With the current P/E modestly below the Fair Ratio, the shares screen as slightly undervalued on this metric.

Result: UNDERVALUED
